title: Heterogeneous RPC Inference (RTX 3090 + Strix Halo)
hypothesis: Splitting Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-REAP-20 Q6_K across the RTX 3090 (24 GB, ~936 GB/s VRAM) and the Strix Halo Framework Desktop (128 GB unified, ~215 GB/s) via llama.cpp RPC can outperform single-node Halo decode by >=15% at 8K context, because the 3090 holds the hot attention layers at CUDA bandwidth while the Halo absorbs the long tail of MoE experts in its huge GTT pool.

gpus_explicitly_excluded: <cuda-peer> CUDA0,2,3,4 ((redacted sibling CUDA device) x4) — pinned out via CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1
scope.rationale_for_exclusion: Per user direction: study the hard case of mixing a mid-tier NVIDIA card with the Strix Halo, not the trivial case where a single Blackwell holds the whole model.

halo.description: Strix Halo Framework Desktop (see AGENTS.md 'Access' section for addresses and keys)
halo.link: Wi-Fi — ethernet interface present but DOWN

<cuda-peer>.description: Companion box with RTX 3090 (see AGENTS.md 'Companion Box' section)
<cuda-peer>.gpu_pin: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=4 (isolates the RTX 3090 from the 4x Blackwell cards on the same host)

method: Build llama.cpp with -DGGML_CUDA=ON -DGGML_RPC=ON -DCMAKE_CUDA_ARCHITECTURES=86 on the 3090 box.
Stage Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-REAP-20-Q6_K.gguf on whichever node drives inference (transfer once if needed).
Sweep: which node is driver (host) x which node is RPC worker x layers-on-3090 in {8,16,20,24,28} x ctx in {4096,16384}.
Measure: TTFT, prefill tok/s, decode tok/s, per-node VRAM/GTT, LAN bandwidth consumed during a run.
Compare against single-node Halo Q6_K baseline already in results.jsonl.
success_criteria: Heterogeneous RPC run beats single-node Halo decode tok/s on Q6_K by >=15% at 8K ctx, OR
Profiling clearly identifies the Wi-Fi link as the dominant bottleneck with measured bandwidth numbers, motivating an ethernet/10GbE upgrade before the next attempt.
